reasoning_summary: For live run 20260421_123908_bf16_gemm_v1_1db08fc at 46.056448 ms, the compact 4-row writer sweep produced only a token 0.128 ms improvement and left the kernel pinned at the same 198 registers/thread, 16.62% active warps, and occupancy_limit_registers=2 as the current correctness-safe anchor. Combined with the previous round, that is enough evidence to stop spending immediate budget on writer-shape variants: the round-7 low-register signal was interesting, but the two correctness-safe follow-ons both converged back to the same old occupancy floor. The next best move is therefore to retime the PTX wait-group and CTA barrier seam on the current correct anchor. I keep one strictly smaller export-address cleanup direction second in case there is still a tiny low-risk scalar win left that does not disturb row coverage, and I keep the 256x128 half-panel pivot third as the orthogonal structural branch once the local PTX seam is exhausted.

family_audit.reason: The writer-family retries flattened out and the current correct anchor still shows barrier plus long-scoreboard as the cleanest local seam.
A smaller scalar cleanup than the recent writer rewrites is still defensible, but it stays below the barrier seam.
Best orthogonal high-upside branch after the local PTX anchor is exhausted.
Two correctness-safe follow-ons converged back to the same 198-register anchor, so this family should pause for now.
The repo is already sitting on the current correctness-safe writer anchor; no extra restore budget is needed this round.

directions.name: Retime the PTX barrier seam on the current correctness-safe 128x128 anchor
directions.hypothesis: The last two writer-family iterations both landed back on the same 198-register / 16.6%-active-warps regime, so the cheapest remaining local seam on the correct anchor is the cp.async wait-group plus CTA barrier handoff. A bounded retime can target the persistent 5.95% barrier stall and 6.85% long-scoreboard stall without touching writer coverage or geometry.
directions.expected_bottleneck: synchronization_barrier_issue layered on top of occupancy_latency_hiding_issue in the current correctness-safe 128x128 PTX anchor

score_breakdown.reason: Best current-anchor exploit after the writer-shape family flattened out.

ranking_notes: This is now the cleanest local seam on the current correctness-safe anchor.
